title: Ops runbook + data-refresh cadence (re-ingest/re-validate/re-sign-off) + outcomes dashboard
context: After the deed is delivered (incidence-explorers-handoff-017), the project needs a durable maintenance structure: a named rotation, a data-refresh cadence tied to upstream releases (SEER, GLOBOCAN, USCS update periodically), and an outcomes dashboard tracking comprehension and adoption — not pageviews. Cancer registry data lags by years and must be refreshed and re-validated on each upstream release. Any changed HIGH-tier patient-facing content must be re-signed-off by the oncologist and advocate (version-scoped sign-off does not carry forward). A gated process for adding the next cancer site must also be documented.
objective: Write the ops runbook covering: deploy process; the data-release refresh cycle (re-ingest on SEER/GLOBOCAN/USCS release, re-validate against the upstream tool, re-date provenance, re-verify helplines, re-run all eval gates); re-sign-off of changed HIGH-tier content (version-scoped sign-off does not carry forward); named maintenance rotation with ownership documented; and a gated, expert-reviewed process for adding the next cancer site. Implement the outcomes dashboard tracking comprehension improvement and partner adoption.
acceptanceCriteria: The ops runbook covers deploy, the data-release refresh cycle (re-ingest, re-validate, re-date provenance, re-verify helplines, re-run all eval gates on each upstream SEER/GLOBOCAN/USCS release), and re-sign-off of any changed HIGH-tier content
The runbook explicitly states that version-scoped expert sign-off does not carry forward to edits — changed patient-facing content requires a new sign-off before it ships
A named maintenance rotation with documented ownership is established
An outcomes dashboard tracks comprehension improvement and partner adoption (not pageviews); it is operational and reviewed on the maintenance cadence
A documented, expert-gated process for adding the next cancer site is included
Reviewed and signed off by the Maintainer, Steward, and Expert (oncologist + advocate) (recorded)
output: A reviewed ops runbook (deploy, data-refresh cadence, re-sign-off rules, maintenance rotation, expansion process) and an operational outcomes dashboard tracking comprehension/adoption — sustaining the project post-delivery with expert-gated guardrails maintained on the refresh cycle.
